Across CHIRAL Bangladesh, DeepBio Academy, and structured mentorship programs, I have trained 3,000+ early-career researchers in computational biology and reproducible research methodology. This work is framed as **scientific capacity building**: the goal is not only to teach analysis skills, but to standardize Open Science practices — version control, containerized environments, and documented pipelines — so that reproducibility becomes a default habit researchers carry into their own labs.

**Bioinformatics Mentorship Program (BMP)** | [mdjubayerhossain.com/bmp](https://mdjubayerhossain.com/bmp)

A 12-week intensive program (8 live sessions, 7 mentees/cohort) designed to transition early-career researchers from data consumers to independent analysts capable of conducting sequencing data analysis and publishing results. Mentees work across two research tracks — cancer genomics and neurogenomics — developing dual expertise in biological interpretation and hands-on computational analysis using R/Python bioinformatics pipelines. Each cohort standardizes on Git/GitHub version control, Docker/Singularity containerization, and fully documented, reproducible code — establishing computational lab culture norms from day one. Program outcomes include manuscript-ready research outputs with reproducible code; mentees report completed projects progressing to peer-reviewed publication.

**Bulk RNA-Seq Meta-Analysis of Liver, Kidney, Pancreatic, and Gastrointestinal Cancers for Biomarker and Immune Signature Discovery** (July 2025 - present)

- **Rahnuma Tabassum** (Biochemistry & Molecular Biology, Jagannath University) - Liver Cancer
- **Fayez Ahmad** (Notre Dame College) - Esophageal Cancer
- **Sabbir Khan** (Doctor of Veterinary Medicine, Gazipur Agricultural University) - Kidney Cancer
- **Md Shakil Ahamed** (Biotechnology & Genetic Engineering, Mawlana Bhashani Science & Technology University) - Colon Cancer
- **Lamisa Manha Aditee** (Biotechnology, BRAC University) - Pancreatic Cancer

**GSA Bioinformatics Internship** | [gsabioinfointernship.owlstown.net](https://gsabioinfointernship.owlstown.net)

A scientific capacity building initiative (5 mentees) bridging theoretical education and real-world research in life sciences through bioinformatics, data science, and molecular biology; organized in partnership with GNOBB, ASI School of Life, and SPSB. Mentors provided by DeepBio Limited, Big Bioinformatics Lab, and CHIRAL Bangladesh; interns gain hands-on access to the GNOBB-ASI Bioinformatics Hub for RNA-Seq, multi-omics, and systems biology analyses, working within a structured reproducible research framework from the outset.
